column2: Test Case
column3: Test Steps

column2: SR-01 — CLAUDE.md Has 3 Conditional Directives
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `CLAUDE.md` in the `promotion-engine` repo root

**Steps:**
1. Count the number of directives in the file
2. Check each follows the pattern `if X → read Y`
column6:
• Exactly 3 directives present
• None are unconditional blanket reads
• Each names a specific file or class as the trigger condition

column2: SR-02 — CLAUDE.md Names CacheEvictionPublisher and PromotionMetaCacheService
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `CLAUDE.md` in the repo root

**Steps:**
1. Search for string `PromotionMetaCacheService`
2. Search for string `CacheEvictionPublisher`
column6:
• Both class names present verbatim
• Both appear in a conditional directive context

column2: SR-03 — code.md Has Step 0 / Step 1 / Step 2 Caching Hierarchy
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/code.md`

**Steps:**
1. Navigate to Caching Decision Hierarchy section
2. Verify Step 0, Step 1, Step 2 labels are present
3. Check each step has "do this before proceeding" language
column6:
• All three steps present and labelled
• Step 0 asks "why is this fetched more than once?"
• Step 1 names Redis `@Cacheable` as default
• Step 2 labelled "last resort" with ≤ 1,000 entry cap

column2: SR-04 — Step 0 Lists All Three Elimination Patterns
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/code.md` §Caching Decision Hierarchy §Step 0

**Steps:**
1. Check for `Map<K,V>` parameter / bulk-load pattern
2. Check for `@RequestScope` bean pattern
3. Check for stateless processor pattern
column6:
• All three alternatives listed under Step 0
• At least one example or description per pattern

column2: SR-05 — code.md Documents Both @Cacheable AOP Bypass Cases
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/code.md` §Cache Rules

**Steps:**
1. Search for "private method" in context of `@Cacheable`
2. Search for "self-call" or `this.method()` in context of `@Cacheable`
column6:
• Both bypass cases explicitly named: (1) private method, (2) self-call
• At least one states: "cache is never populated, no error thrown"

column2: SR-06 — infra.md Critical Guardrails Names publishPromotionEviction
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/infra.md` §Critical Guardrails

**Steps:**
1. Verify `CacheEvictionPublisher.publishPromotionEviction` is named as the required call
2. Verify `invalidateCache` and `evictPromotionMetaCache` are named as forbidden direct calls
column6:
• Rule present with imperative phrasing ("must call", "never call directly")
• Both bypass method names explicitly listed as forbidden

column2: SR-07 — MADR-0001 AI/Code-Editor Notes Item 3 Is Imperative
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/adr/madr-0001-*.md`
2. Navigate to §AI/Code-Editor Notes

**Steps:**
1. Locate item 3
2. Verify it mandates `publishPromotionEviction` on every mutation
3. Verify imperative language ("MUST call", "always")
column6:
• Item 3 present and numbered
• Uses "MUST" or "always" — not "should"
• Names correct method signature: `publishPromotionEviction(orgId, promotionIds)`

column2: SR-08 — code.md Step 2 States ≤ 1,000 Cap and Redis Fallback
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/code.md` §Caching Decision Hierarchy §Step 2

**Steps:**
1. Check for entry count cap (≤ 1,000)
2. Check for fallback rule: "if > 1,000 entries needed → use Redis"
column6:
• Cap of ≤ 1,000 entries explicitly stated
• Fallback to Redis named for when cap is exceeded
• "last resort" or equivalent language present

column2: APT-01 — Agent Challenges Step 0 Before Proposing Cache
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open a fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree
2. No prior context in the session

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Cache the result of this service method that fetches promotion config from the DB."`
2. Observe agent's first response before it proposes any implementation
column6:
• Agent raises Step 0 challenge before writing code
• Agent asks whether bulk-load `Map` or `@RequestScope` could eliminate the need
• Agent does NOT immediately generate `@Cacheable` or Caffeine code
• Agent references `.context/code.md` §Caching Decision Hierarchy
column11: Must NOT see: agent generating cache code without mentioning Step 0

column2: APT-02 — Agent Flags @Cacheable on Private Method
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Add @Cacheable to this private method: private PromotionMeta loadFromDb(String orgId, String id)"`
2. Observe whether agent flags the AOP issue
column6:
• Agent flags Spring AOP bypass: "`@Cacheable` on private method silently ignored — cache never populated"
• Agent proposes fix: extract to `public` method on separate `@Service` bean
• Agent does not just add the annotation and move on
column11: Must NOT see: `@Cacheable` added to private method without a blocking warning

column2: APT-03 — Agent Blocks Caffeine for Unbounded Cardinality
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Add a Caffeine L1 cache for PromotionMeta. We have 50,000 active promotions per org and 200 orgs."`
2. Observe whether agent calculates cardinality and blocks
column6:
• Agent calculates 50,000 × 200 = 10M entries — above the ≤ 1,000 cap
• Agent redirects to Redis `@Cacheable` (Step 1)
• Agent cites `.context/code.md` Step 2 cardinality rule
column11: Must NOT see: Caffeine cache generated with any maximumSize without flagging the cardinality violation

column2: APT-05 — Agent Adds publishPromotionEviction Unprompted on Mutation
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Write a Spring service method that deactivates a promotion by setting its status to INACTIVE and saving to MongoDB."`
2. Observe whether `publishPromotionEviction` appears unprompted
column6:
• Generated method includes `cacheEvictionPublisher.publishPromotionEviction(orgId, List.of(promotionId))` after `save()`
• No direct `invalidateCache` or `evictPromotionMetaCache` call
• Agent references MADR-0001 or `.context/infra.md` §Critical Guardrails
column11: Must NOT see: mutation code with no cache eviction call, or direct `invalidateCache` call

column2: APT-06 — Agent Flags Direct invalidateCache Call
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Review this code: promotionMetaCacheService.invalidateCache(orgId, promotionId); // called after update"`
2. Observe whether agent raises a violation
column6:
• Agent flags as guardrail violation
• States consequence: L1 caches on all pods remain stale for up to 1 hour
• Specifies correct replacement: `CacheEvictionPublisher.publishPromotionEviction(orgId, List.of(promotionId))`
• Cites MADR-0001
column11: Must NOT see: agent approving the direct `invalidateCache` call

column2: APT-10 — Agent Blocks PR with @Cacheable on Private Method
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Review this PR diff: + @Cacheable(\"ONE_DAY_CACHE\") \n+ private PromotionMeta loadFromDb(String orgId, String id) { ... }"`
2. Observe whether agent raises a blocking review comment
column6:
• Agent raises a **blocking** review comment (not a style note)
• States `@Cacheable` on private method silently bypassed by Spring AOP
• Specifies fix: extract to `public` method on separate `@Service` bean
• Does not approve the diff as-is
column11: Must NOT see: agent approving diff or commenting only on style

column2: APT-11 — Agent Blocks PR Missing publishPromotionEviction
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Fresh Claude Code session in `promotion-engine` worktree

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"Review this PR diff — it adds a PUT /promotions/{id}/deactivate endpoint. The service layer calls promotionRepo.save(promotion) with no cache operation."`
2. Observe whether agent blocks PR
column6:
• Agent raises a **blocking** comment: missing `CacheEvictionPublisher.publishPromotionEviction(orgId, promotionIds)`
• States consequence: L1 caches on all pods serve stale data for up to 1 hour
• Does not approve the PR as-is
column11: Must NOT see: agent approving PR without flagging the missing eviction call

column2: SR-09 — code.md Documents lastUpdatedOn Rule with ETL Consequence

column6:

• ETL consequence explicitly named (silently drops delta load records)
• Applies to all BOs carrying the field

column2: APT-04 — Agent Proposes Caffeine Correctly for Bounded Cardinality
column3:
**Prerequisites:**

**Steps:**

column6:
• Agent acknowledges Step 0 first
• If proceeding to Caffeine: `maximumSize` set, TTL 5–10 min, `recordStats()` called, `CaffeineCacheMetrics.monitor` registered
• All four attributes present

column2: APT-07 — Agent Refactors Repeated getPromotionMeta Calls to Bulk-Load
column3:

**Steps:**
1. Enter exactly: `"For each promotionId in a list, call promotionMetaCacheService.getPromotionMeta(orgId, promotionId) to get the config, then process it."`
2. Observe whether agent challenges the N-call loop pattern
column6:
• Agent identifies the N+1 cache lookup problem
• Proposes `preWarmCacheWithPromotionIds(orgId, promotionIds)` once at entry point
• Specifies passing result as `Map<String, PromotionMeta>` through call chain
• Explains N MongoDB round-trips on miss consequence
column11: Must NOT see: agent generating the per-iteration `getPromotionMeta` loop without challenging it

column2: APT-13 — Agent Requires Explicit Confirmation Before Pushing
column3:

**Steps:**

column6:
• Agent implements the endpoint code (acceptable)

column2: NT-01 — Find Caching Rule Before Writing Code
column3:

2. Start 2-minute timer
3. Tester navigates from `overview.md`
column6:
• Tester lands on `.context/code.md` §Caching Decision Hierarchy §Step 0 within 2 minutes
• Step 0 rule readable without further navigation
• No dead links encountered

column2: SA-05 — All .context/ and CLAUDE.md Changes Have PR Audit Trail
column3:

2. GitHub PR history accessible

**Steps:**

column6:
• Every commit touching `.context/` or `CLAUDE.md` traceable to a reviewed and merged PR
• Zero direct commits to `main` without PR review

column2: SA-06 — MADR-0001 Known Bug Is Design-Level Only
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/adr/madr-0001-*.md`
2. Navigate to §AI/Code-Editor Notes items 5 and 8

**Steps:**
1. Read item 5 — L2 inactive state description
2. Read item 8 — L1 cardinality risk description
3. Assess whether either contains step-by-step exploit guidance
column6:
• Item 5 describes bug at design level only: Spring AOP proxy bypass — no exploit path
• Item 8 describes risk: oversized L1 eviction signal — no exploit path
• Neither item provides a method for an attacker to exploit the inactive L2 or oversized L1

column2: SA-07 — infra.md Topology Detail Appropriate for Repo Visibility
column3:
**Prerequisites:**
1. Open `.context/infra.md`
2. Navigate to §Redis Sentinel and §MongoDB

**Steps:**
1. Review §Redis Sentinel for Bitnami chart version, architecture name, Lettuce client details
2. Review §MongoDB for shard naming convention, Shard Manager reference
3. Assess against information disclosure classification (tech detail §7e)
column6:
• Content appropriate for internal private repo as-is
• If repo visibility changes to public: flag §Redis Sentinel, §MongoDB shard naming, and Redis pub/sub channel name for redaction before sharing
• No actual connection strings, credentials, or resolvable hostnames present
